description: Adjudicated genuine_wrong sites from the CDO L3 semantic audit (2026-06-30, re-verified 2026-07-01 post beyond-1B.3b Tasks 1-2): call sites where the fresh resolver and the L3 oracle confidently resolve to DISJOINT targets. The first 42 entries: fresh classifies the call as a platform builtin (kind=255: e.g. message, confirm, clear, strlen, copystr, delchr, evaluate, strsubstno, PageInstance::setselectionfilter/update/close/runmodal, Record::setrecfilter/fieldno, RecordRef::open); L3 emits a source-procedure target for the same callee text. DIRECTIONALITY RESOLVED (beyond-1B.3b Task 3): every one of these 42 entries was independently adjudicated by opening the CDO_WS source at the site, confirming (a) the callee is a genuine platform-intrinsic call shape (bare global call, or a member call on a Record/RecordRef/Page-instance receiver), (b) the claimed name+receiver-kind is a real member of the structural builtin catalog (`builtins::is_global_builtin` / `member_catalog::member_builtin`), and (c) NO competing same-name source `procedure` is declared in the same unit (the Task-1 lookup-precedence shadow check) — grep-verified zero matches across all 42 sites. Verdict for all 42: `l3_error_intrinsic`. Entries 43-44 (beyond-1B.3b Task 5.5, added when wiring the implicit Base App dependency into the closure surfaced these 2 NEWLY-REACHABLE cross-app sites for the first time): fresh resolves each call, WITH SOURCE EVIDENCE, to a real procedure declared in Microsoft Base Application's own embedded (ShowMyCode) source — independently verified by extracting and reading that source directly from the real `.app` — while L3's frozen golden pairs the SAME site/callee_fp with an unrelated target (a different call's target entirely, or a nested-event's subscriber set several hops downstream of the actual callee). Entries 45-51 (follow-up plan v2.1 Task 3, `cause: cross-app-source-bare-implicit-rec`, added when `resolve_bare`'s Step 3 — bare unqualified calls implicitly dispatching to `Rec` in Page/Table/TableExtension/PageExtension code — was implemented): 7 distinct Pages whose bare `Navigate();` action call (no receiver at all — an even more indirect shape than entries 43-44's qualified member calls) now correctly resolves to their respective `SourceTable`'s own `Navigate` procedure, a REAL `procedure Navigate()` independently re-verified in Microsoft Base Application's embedded ShowMyCode source for each of the 7 distinct target tables (Return Receipt Header/Issued Fin. Charge Memo Header/Service Cr.Memo Header/Service Invoice Header/Sales Cr.Memo Header/Sales Shipment Header/Service Shipment Header) — the standard 'open the Navigate page for this posted document' pattern Base App ships on every posted-document-header table. L3's frozen golden predates bare-implicit-Rec dispatch entirely (the fresh resolver did not implement it yet when the golden was minted), so it never modeled this shape and pairs each site with an unrelated target instead. Verdict for entries 1-51: `l3_error_intrinsic` (fresh is CORRECT; an L3 golden defect, not a fresh bug). Entry 52 (record-field chains plan Task 4, added when `infer_receiver_type`'s new Step 3a — bare implicit-Rec QUOTED-field receivers, `"Field".X()` with no `Rec.` prefix — was implemented, `cause: builtin-catalog-fp-collision`, the SAME site/routine as entries 39-40): `Table 6175281 "CDO Setup"` field(203; "Azure Blob Private Endpoint URL"; Text[250])'s own `OnValidate` trigger, `"Azure Blob Private Endpoint URL".Trim()` — the SAME statement (`CopyStr("...".Trim(), 1, MaxStrLen("..."))`) as entries 39-40's `CopyStr`/`MaxStrLen`, now resolving its THIRD call (the nested `.Trim()` member call on the bare quoted Text field) for the first time. Fresh classifies it `Text::trim` (kind=255, a genuine Text-framework catalog member — `member_catalog::member_builtin(Framework(Text), "trim")`); no `procedure Trim(` is declared anywhere in this unit (grep-verified). L3's frozen golden pairs this callee_fp with `Table 6175281.CheckAzureContainerPerCompany` — an UNRELATED procedure called from a DIFFERENT field's `OnValidate` trigger 8-31 lines away (lines 300/324) — the identical L3 line/routine-key misattribution bug already documented for entries 39-40 on this exact line, now also exposed on this third callee_fp. Verdict: `l3_error_intrinsic`. Entries 53-54 (argtype-dispatch-and-page-catalog plan, Task 1, `cause: builtin-catalog-fp-collision-duplicate-trigger-name`, added when the Page/Report instance-catalog completion made `Page.SetTableView` resolve on general declared Page-typed variables — not just the `CurrPage`/`Page` singleton — for the first time): `Page 6175276 "CDO E-Mail Template Card"` and `Page 6175425 "CDO Template Recipient Setup"` each declare TWO SEPARATE field controls with their own `trigger OnAssistEdit()` (a legal, common AL pattern — every editable field can have its own assist-edit trigger). Fresh's `RoutineNodeId` disambiguates the two same-named triggers via `enclosing_member_lc` (the owning field's name) and correctly resolves the flagged site's own statement — `FieldsWithRelationPage.SetTableView(Field)`, a `Page "CDO Fields (With Relation)"`-typed local variable's PAGE_INSTANCE catalog member (no `procedure SetTableView(` declared in either unit) — but L3's frozen `(unit, line, callee_fp)` golden site key carries no such per-control qualifier for `from_routine_lc=onassistedit`, so it mis-pairs this site's target with the OTHER same-named trigger's unrelated call (`ReportSelectionUsageLookup()` on Page 6175276's earlier `Report Selection Usage` field trigger; `LineAssistEdit()` on Page 6175425's earlier `Description` field trigger) — the SAME `builtin-catalog-fp-collision` class of L3 site-misattribution documented for entries 3-4/39-42/52 above (a duplicate-trigger-name variant of the general phenomenon), now exposed for the first time because fresh previously emitted NO target at all for either site (Task 1's predecessor state: `SetTableView` on a general Page variable stayed `Unknown(MemberNotFound)`, so the comparison never reached the target-mismatch path). Verdict: `l3_error_intrinsic`. Zero `fresh_false_builtin`, zero `needs_manual_review` survivors across all 54. The corrections are recorded as a SEPARATE overlay, `tests/goldens/semantic-edges/adjudicated-overrides.json` (canonical catalog keys / independently-verified cross-app routine targets + source_sha256, never a copy of any fresh-computed edge/target id) — `cdo-anon.json` (the frozen L3 golden) itself is left UNTOUCHED; `run_cdo_semantic_audit` applies the overlay IN-MEMORY before diffing fresh. This manifest remains the historical adjudication record AND the bounded set-membership gate: any FUTURE genuine_wrong site not enumerated here still fails the gate immediately. Entry 9 (0-indexed; pageext-merge-and-final-residual plan, Task 2): CDOEMailJobs.Page.al (Page 6175294, SourceTable = "CDO E-Mail Job") RunAction's OnAction trigger calls bare Run(); (no receiver). Task 2 grounded Run (and 18 sibling PAGE_INSTANCE names) as having NO bare-call form anywhere in AL (MS Learn citations in resolver::INSTANCE_ONLY_NEVER_BARE), so the resolve_bare Step 3 PROBE-THEN-DECIDE collision guard no longer treats run as a competing global/page-instance reading here: the call now correctly resolves, WITH SOURCE EVIDENCE, to the SourceTable Table 6175280 "CDO E-Mail Job"'s own procedure Run() (independently re-verified directly in the live CDO_WS source, same app as the caller — never a compiled .alpackages dependency, hence the new receiver_kind, SameAppSourceProcedure, distinct from entries 43-51's CrossAppSourceProcedure). This entry was one of the ORIGINAL 42 (previously adjudicated fresh_builtin: run / cause: builtin-catalog-fp-collision, when fresh's own pre-Task-2 disposition for this site WAS Catalog/Builtin(run) because that adjudication only inspected the SAME UNIT for a competing procedure Run( — never the page's own SourceTable, a DIFFERENT file/object) and was CORRECTED IN PLACE (same site key; unit/line/callee_fp unchanged; entry count stays 54, not 55 — an earlier draft of this correction briefly described this as a new 'Entry 55' before being caught and fixed in place, per task-2-report.md's own 'first attempt was wrong' note). Verdict remains l3_error_intrinsic. Zero fresh_false_builtin, zero needs_manual_review survivors across all 54. CORRECTION (pageext-merge-and-final-residual plan, Task 2 REVIEW fix, dated 2026-07-04): the paragraph above (as originally written during Task 2) additionally claimed that "L3's frozen golden ... still pairs this site/callee_fp with the OLD Builtin(run) reading, which is now itself the error being corrected for" — FALSE. An independent HMAC-based re-verification of the RAW, un-overridden L3 golden (cdo-anon.json) for this exact site established that its recorded target has ALWAYS been Table 6175280 "CDO E-Mail Job".Run — IDENTICAL to fresh's post-Task-2 resolution above. L3 was never wrong here; the actual (now-closed) defect was fresh's OWN pre-Task-2 answer (Catalog/Builtin(run)), which Task 2 fixed for an unrelated reason (the INSTANCE_ONLY_NEVER_BARE grounding). This site is consequently now a plain MATCH between fresh and the raw L3 golden: the adjudicated-overrides.json override for this key is a documented NO-OP (it writes back the exact target the raw golden already supplies), and removing the override would not change run_cdo_semantic_audit's outcome. The entry is RETAINED here (not deleted) as the historical adjudication record, per this manifest's own stated purpose ('remains the historical adjudication record AND the bounded set-membership gate') and this project's errata-over-erasure convention (corrections are appended in place, not silently rewritten) — deleting it would also force renumbering every prose-labelled entry range below it (43-44/45-51/52/53-54 are positional, not just chronological, labels), a needless and error-prone rewrite for a purely cosmetic gain. Entry count is unaffected either way: still 54.

entries.unit: Al/Page/Page 6175389 CDO Local Print Service Part.al

entries.fresh_target: Table 304 "Issued Fin. Charge Memo Header".Navigate (Base Application, source-verified)
